rationale.R1.2: Several logical issues warrant attention: Hoeffding (1963) is cited as the warrant for conditional independence across calls, but that paper establishes concentration inequalities for bounded random variables — conditional independence is a separate modelling assumption that requires its own justification; the continuous relaxation of the integer variable k is invoked without acknowledging the integrality gap or bounding the rounding error; the monotonicity claim ('easier queries should receive more calls') is described as counter-intuitive but its derivation is not shown, so correctness cannot be verified from the text.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing for any 'improve' delegation. The draft mixes informal in-line notation (p-bar, k*) with display-math style inconsistently; several sentences are syntactically overloaded (e.g. the sentence beginning 'Differentiating the Lagrangian'); the phrase 'pins k* to the inverse of the binomial-tail derivative' is obscure; and the concluding paragraph's hedge chain ('which we adopt as the operating regime') weakens the empirical claim.
rationale.R1.7: Two citation issues are potentially load-bearing: (1) Hoeffding (1963) is misattributed as the source of conditional independence rather than as a concentration-inequality reference; (2) Brown et al. (2020) (GPT-3) is described as representative of 'modern compound-system per-call performance' — a 2020 paper calibrating a different architecture is a thin warrant for a 2026 operating regime, and a more current citation (or explicit caveat) is needed.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement address technical and logical correctness (e.g., the Hoeffding misattribution, the continuous-relaxation argument, the unproven monotonicity claim) in addition to prose clarity, or is the scope limited to writing quality and presentation only?
